title: Name age and monastic rank remain unresolved
source_observation: Current evidence identifies an elderly revered Buddhist teacher but does not fix one spelling age rank lineage or institution
risk: Casting costume and publicity could invent a complete historical biography or standard monastic office
decision: Keep terminology provisional avoid exact age claims and approve costume and institutional language only after Tamil and Buddhist-historical review
rationale: Protects textual honesty and prevents imported monastic systems
required_review: Classical Tamil; Buddhist history; casting; costume; publicity

title: Aravana listens before interpreting
source_observation: Manimekalai gives an extended account of the garden Manipallavam previous births mantras and vessel before he teaches
risk: The mentor could interrupt with omniscience and reduce the heroine to a passive recipient
decision: Give the account full dramatic space and make his response depend on what he has heard directly
rationale: Preserves testimony knowledge continuity and the student's agency

title: Age is embodied history not comic weakness or automatic omniscience
source_observation: The text describes white hair and trembling speech while showing sustained travel intervention memory and teaching
risk: Old-age shorthand could make him feeble mystical or unquestionable
decision: Show physical cost recovery and deliberate speech alongside alert judgement and permit uncertainty or error
rationale: Creates a playable elder without ableist or devotional caricature
required_review: Actor preparation; ageing specialist; disability review; director

title: Mentor authority does not own the student's conscience
source_observation: Aravana guides Manimekalai at several transitions but she also receives revelation serves travels questions and examines rival schools
risk: The adaptation could make all her choices extensions of a male teacher's will
decision: Let him equip correct and challenge while preserving her right to question interpret choose and act beyond his presence
rationale: Protects the protagonist's mature agency and strengthens the mentor relationship
required_review: Screenplay; gender studies; Buddhist studies; performance

title: Narrative teaching answers a present ethical question
source_observation: Aravana uses previous-birth and Aputhiran narratives in response to grief revelation the vessel and hunger
risk: Branch stories could become detachable illustrated exposition
decision: Every retained narrative must change a decision understanding or action in the receiving scene
rationale: Makes story causally necessary and reduces lecture structure
required_review: Adaptation editor; classical Tamil; screenplay; story department

title: Service and hunger precede abstract doctrinal display
source_observation: The vessel teaching directs Manimekalai into public feeding before the final formal logic instruction
risk: The mentor could be remembered only as a doctrinal lecturer while the epic's social ethics disappear
decision: Stage his authority first through the demand that abundance serve hungry beings and later connect reasoning to lived suffering
rationale: Keeps compassion action and philosophy integrated
required_review: Food and hunger research; Buddhist studies; screenplay; editor

title: Palace intervention uses moral and social authority not magical command
source_observation: Madhavi and Sudhamathi seek Aravana's help during Manimekalai's custody and he contributes to release
risk: A holy-man entrance could instantly override the queen and erase institutions testimony and political risk
decision: Show access reporting questioning reputation and negotiation and identify what action changes custody
rationale: Preserves the political stakes and prevents deus-ex-machina authority
required_review: Classical Tamil; legal history; politics; screenplay

title: Buddhist conviction remains explicit without caricaturing rivals
source_observation: Aravana teaches Buddhist dharma after Manimekalai has heard competing philosophical positions
risk: Generic spirituality could erase the epic's standpoint while sectarian staging could make opponents foolish or corrupt
decision: Name and dramatise his Buddhist commitments while allowing rival arguments intellectual dignity and genuine questions
rationale: Preserves the work's religious identity and the seriousness of inquiry
required_review: Buddhist studies; history of religions; classical Tamil; dialogue

title: Logic instruction is dialogic concrete and dramatically earned
source_observation: Chapter 29 presents structured teaching on valid knowledge perception inference and argument after Manimekalai requests clarification
risk: The climax could become a static technical lecture or simplify logic into slogans
decision: Use a concrete case student formulation correction and visible reasoning while preserving terminology for specialist review
rationale: Makes intellectual growth cinematic without falsifying doctrine
required_review: Buddhist logic; classical Tamil; dialogue; actor rehearsal; editor

title: Aravana is not an infallible mouthpiece beyond interpretation or review
source_observation: Reverence surrounds the teacher and his speech includes karma gender social judgement and sectarian claims from a particular literary world
risk: The film could treat every statement as timeless fact or avoid the text by making him generically modern
decision: Preserve relevant teaching with context listener response consequences and explicit uncertainty where scholarship is unresolved
rationale: Allows serious Buddhist commitment without erasing ethical and historical complexity
required_review: Classical Tamil; Buddhist studies; gender studies; ethics; sensitivity review
